title: RFC 9309 Robots Semantics and Library Conformance Adapter · Type Recall
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs rfc 9309 robots semantics and library conformance adapter from revision, authorization, seed, URL, policy, response bytes, parsed identity, record provenance, frontier transition, Promise settlement, browser state, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Construct the RFC 9309 retrieval URL from the target service origin and send one stable truthful crawler product token with an accountable contact.
Apply group selection, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior from RFC 9309.
Classify successful, redirecting, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, and stale robots responses conservatively.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 4805 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove RFC 9309 product, group, encoding, longest-match, response-state, cache, and library adapter behavior; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat crawl-delay, request-rate, sitemap, and other extensions as documented policy inputs without claiming RFC 9309 requires them.

options.text: Treat crawl-delay, request-rate, sitemap, and other extensions as documented policy inputs without claiming RFC 9309 requires them. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser.
crawl-delay is a normative RFC 9309 directive with universal behavior. That claim fails the extension restraint · changed case evidence boundary for Extension Restraint.

For extension restraint · changed case, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Extension Restraint for RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser.

content.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 4091 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove RFC 9309 product, group, encoding, longest-match, response-state, cache, and library adapter behavior; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Construct the RFC 9309 retrieval URL from the target service origin and send one stable truthful crawler product token with an accountable contact.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat crawl-delay, request-rate, sitemap, and other extensions as documented policy inputs without claiming RFC 9309 requires them.
